interactions.summary: Jiang defines collective mythology as the force that drives history when authority breaks down, then reads Napoleon's St. Helena religious fantasy as a confession of his method.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang, with quoted/read Napoleon material in seg-0034 and seg-0035.

interactions.summary: He reviews old-regime French army dysfunction, Carnot's conscription system, Robespierre's too-demanding virtue, and Napoleon's talent for finding patrons after Robespierre's fall.

speaker_notes.note: Jiang is quoting or paraphrasing Napoleon's St. Helena reflection about founding a religion and composing a new Koran.

claims.claim: When collective authority breaks down, shared mythologies begin to drive history and individuals can capture attention by acting out leading roles.
claims.temporal_scope: General historical model in this lecture.

claims.claim: Napoleon understood the French Revolution as a crusade and tried to become its Muhammad, Caesar, Alexander, and Jesus figure.
Napoleon built official history through paintings, stories, and a cult of personality, so accounts of his victories require suspicion.
Carnot created levee en masse to dilute noble influence, but later conspired against Robespierre because Robespierre's virtue prevented beneficiaries from enjoying power.
Napoleon's military distinctiveness was political patronage: he identified powerful patrons and used favors to advance his career.

signature_moments.moment: Remove the teachers and no one becomes in charge; mythology takes charge.
signature_moments.source_phrase: when you remove your teachers it doesn't mean no it's in charge it just means that now these mythology are in charge
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It states Jiang's political psychology of authority collapse.

signature_moments.moment: Napoleon dreams of founding a religion, riding an elephant into Asia, and carrying a new Koran written to suit his needs.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the lecture's sharpest evidence that Napoleon understands politics as religious staging.

signature_moments.moment: Robespierre's virtue becomes politically dangerous because he demands sacrifice even from the people he promoted.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Robespierre was too virtuous. He demanded on everyone making sacrifices.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains why the system-builder is betrayed by his own beneficiaries.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The layer of shared mythology that governs society once ordinary authority breaks down.
Carnot's mass-conscription system, described as getting everyone into the military to dilute noble influence.
Napoleon's use of paintings, stories, and official history to make perception more important than underlying reality.

chronology_notes.note: Napoleon's St. Helena exile reflection is used as retrospective evidence of his political-religious imagination.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript renders Barras as Paul Barat and Sieyes approximately as Csajas; the political patronage structure is clear enough for semantic extraction.
